2603.29387.title: Extend3D: Town-Scale 3D Generation
2603.29387.paper_summary: In this paper, we propose Extend3D, a training-free pipeline for 3D scene generation from a single image, built upon an object-centric 3D generative model. To overcome the limitations of fixed-size latent spaces in object-centric models for representing wide scenes, we extend the latent space in the $x$ and $y$ directions. Then, by dividing the extended latent space into overlapping patches, we apply the object-centric 3D generative model to each patch and couple them at each time step. Since patch-wise 3D generation with image conditioning requires strict spatial alignment between image and latent patches, we initialize the scene using a point cloud prior from a monocular depth estimator and iteratively refine occluded regions through SDEdit. We discovered that treating the incompleteness of 3D structure as noise during 3D refinement enables 3D completion via a concept, which we term under-noising. Furthermore, to address the sub-optimality of object-centric models for sub-scene generation, we optimize the extended latent during denoising, ensuring that the denoising trajectories remain consistent with the sub-scene dynamics. To this end, we introduce 3D-aware optimization objectives for improved geometric structure and texture fidelity. We demonstrate that our method yields better results than prior methods, as evidenced by human preference and quantitative experiments.
2603.29387.paper_summary_zh: 在本文中，我們提出了 Extend3D，這是一種無需訓練的管道，用於從單一圖像生成 3D 場景，基於以物體為中心的 3D 生成模型。為了克服以物體為中心的模型在表示廣泛場景時固定大小潛在空間的限制，我們在 $x$ 和 $y$ 方向上擴展潛在空間。然後，通過將擴展的潛在空間劃分為重疊的區塊，我們將以物體為中心的 3D 生成模型應用於每個區塊並在每個時間步進行耦合。由於基於圖像條件的區塊式 3D 生成需要圖像和潛在區塊之間的嚴格空間對齊，我們使用單眼深度估計器的點雲先驗來初始化場景，並通過 SDEdit 迭代地細化被遮擋的區域。我們發現，在 3D 精煉過程中將 3D 結構的不完整性視為噪聲，使得通過一個概念實現 3D 完成，這個概念我們稱之為去噪。此外，為了解決以物體為中心的模型在子場景生成中的次優性，我們在去噪過程中優化擴展的潛在空間，確保去噪軌跡與子場景動態保持一致。為此，我們引入了 3D 感知的優化目標，以改善幾何結構和紋理保真度。我們展示了我們的方法比之前的方法產生更好的結果，這在人的偏好和定量實驗中得到了證明。
2603.29387.author: Seungwoo Yoon et.al.
2603.29387.authors: Seungwoo Yoon, Jinmo Kim, Jaesik Park
